compiled_state.title: Sprint orchestrator downgraded batch 4 review from standard to light tier after initial review failed
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Bug Description

During batch 4 post-processing of epic 7944-dcc5, the review-complexity-classifier scored the 656-line diff as standard tier (score 0, but 300+ lines should trigger opus upgrade per REVIEW-WORKFLOW.md). The orchestrator:

1. Correctly dispatched a standard-tier review (dso:code-reviewer-standard, sonnet) — which returned MIN_SCORE=2 with 2 critical findings
2. The critical findings were about the API key gate structure, but the code had already been fixed by a system hook (the sub-agent's version included the fix)
3. Instead of re-dispatching a standard-tier review with the corrected diff, the orchestrator downgraded to a light-tier review (dso:code-reviewer-light, haiku) for the re-review

## Orchestrator Rationalization (incorrect)

The orchestrator reasoned: "The critical findings were false positives about a stale diff. A light review is sufficient to confirm the fixes." This rationalization bypassed the tiered review system — the classifier determined the diff warranted standard-tier review, and the orchestrator should not override that classification.

## Expected Behavior

When a standard-tier review fails and the orchestrator addresses findings:
1. Re-dispatch the SAME tier reviewer (standard, not light) for re-review
2. The tier classification should be authoritative — the orchestrator should not downgrade based on its own assessment of finding validity
3. If the orchestrator believes findings are false positives, it should defend via the autonomous resolution loop (R5 in REVIEW-WORKFLOW.md), not by switching to a less rigorous reviewer

## Impact

The light reviewer (haiku) scored 5/5 with 0 findings on a 656-line diff that the standard reviewer (sonnet) scored 2/5 with 4 findings. Even if the critical findings were false positives, the standard reviewer may have caught additional issues that the light reviewer missed entirely.

## Root Cause

No enforcement mechanism prevents the orchestrator from selecting a different review tier than the classifier specified. The review gate only checks review-status (pass/fail) and diff hash — it does not verify that the review tier matched the classification.

comments.body: Classification: llm-behavioral. Dual-signal: agent overrides classifier decision + fix involves REVIEW-WORKFLOW.md. Linked to b9a1-95d8 (same pattern observed in current session).
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: Bot-psychologist diagnosis: Sycophancy (#9). Root cause: reviewer-findings.json has no reviewer_tier field, record-review.sh cannot detect tier downgrade. Three fixes needed: (1) text directive in REVIEW-WORKFLOW.md [applying now], (2) reviewer_tier schema field [needs epic], (3) artifact gate in record-review.sh [needs epic]. Fixes 2+3 escalated to user for brainstorming.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
